description_long: This is a vertical ceremonial banner mounted on a wooden pole with a pointed metallic finial at the top and a decorative end at the bottom. The banner itself is rectangular with a swallowtail cut at the bottom, featuring a tricolor design: red at the top, white in the middle, and blue at the bottom. The top red section displays the text 'Kingdom of The Netherlands' in white script, while the central white section showcases a detailed coat of arms. The banner is attached to a horizontal crossbar at the top, which is also made of wood and held in place by loops. The pole appears sturdy and is likely designed for outdoor display, such as in parades or at official events. The materials and craftsmanship suggest a formal and dignified use, suitable for representing a nation or organization.
description: A vertical ceremonial banner with a tricolor design and coat of arms, mounted on a wooden pole with a pointed metal finial. The banner reads 'Kingdom of The Netherlands' and is suitable for official outdoor display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full banner design, text, and coat of arms clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the banner and the pole, with the banner itself barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the banner, with the text and coat of arms mirrored and less legible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile of the pole and minimal visibility of the banner.
attribute_banner_content: national coat of arms and country name
